components.title: VPN Remote Access Service
components.description: The VPN (Virtual Private Network) remote access service provides encrypted tunnelled connectivity for authorised personnel accessing organisational systems from outside the office network. Implemented using an SSL/IPsec VPN gateway (Cisco AnyConnect / Palo Alto GlobalProtect / Microsoft Always On VPN or equivalent). All remote access traffic is inspected by the organisation's security controls before reaching internal resources. Split tunnelling is disabled — all traffic from the VPN client routes through the organisation's security stack.

props.name: authentication
props.value: Certificate + MFA via Entra ID / SAML SSO

props.name: split-tunnelling
props.value: Disabled — all traffic routed through organisational security controls

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS / DTLS — SSL VPN primary connection (AnyConnect/GlobalProtect)

port-ranges.remarks: DTLS — UDP-based TLS for improved VPN performance

protocols.name: ipsec-ike
protocols.title: IKEv2 — IPsec VPN key exchange (if IPsec mode is used)

protocols.name: radius
protocols.title: RADIUS — VPN authentication to NPS/RADIUS server

protocols.name: ldaps
protocols.title: LDAPS — Active Directory group membership check for access control

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — VPN connection and authentication event forwarding to SIEM

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for VPN Remote Access Service.

implemented-requirements.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for all privileged remote access via VPN. MFA is enforced at the VPN gateway via SAML SSO integration with Microsoft Entra ID, which applies Conditional Access policies requiring phishing-resistant MFA (Windows Hello for Business or FIDO2) before issuing a VPN session token.

Multi-factor authentication is required for all unprivileged remote access via VPN. MFA is enforced at the Entra ID identity provider level via Conditional Access, applied before the VPN session is established. Single-factor VPN access is not permitted for any user or service account.

VPN access is granted on the principle of least privilege. Network access policies on the VPN gateway restrict which internal resources each user group can reach based on their role. Split tunnelling is disabled — all traffic from remote sessions is inspected by the organisation's firewall, proxy, and security controls.

All VPN connection events (connection established, authentication success/failure, connection duration, source IP, assigned VPN IP, data transferred) are logged and forwarded to the SIEM. Anomalous patterns (unusual hours, impossible travel, high data transfer volumes) trigger security alerts.
